endboard: Mirrorjade + Lost + Banishment (3 Disruptions)
notes: The most basic set-up. This is your classic bread and butter. Often not enough to win the game by itself, but it is solid.

endboard: Dead Mirrorjade + Lost + Quem + Cartesia + Albaz in hand + Grang in gy (4+ Disruptions)
notes: Uses mirrorjade on turn 1, so its not live on the opponents turn.

endboard: Dragostapalia + Requiem Lock (1 Disruption + Floodgate)
notes: Requires Sanctifire, Dragostapelia, 2 Granguinols, and Requiem, and branded sword.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Lost + Quem + Ecclesia + White Albaz + Cartesia in hand + Opening in gy (4+ Disruptions)
notes: Uses the new support cards. Stronger than Aluber + 1 discard combos.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Etude + Bystial Lubellion + Quem + White Albaz in gy. (4+ Disruptions + Floodgate)
notes: Etude floodgate is live since bystial lubellion is one the field.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Etude + Quem + White Albaz + Ecclesia + Cartesia in hand (4+ Disrutptions)
notes: Uses Etude to Synchro Summon on the opponents turn.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Ecclesia + Quem + White Albaz (2+ Disruptions)
notes: Best aluber 1 card combo if you dont play the bystial lubellion.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Lost + Banishment (3 Disruptions)
notes: Sends tragedy with branded fusion, which searches for Aluber.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Etude + Bystial Lubellion + Quem + White Albaz + Ecclesia + Cartesia in hand (4+ Disruptions + Floodgate)
notes: Exactly the same as the Aluber + 2 Discards (version 3), except also has the Etude floodgate live since Bystial Lubellion is still on the field.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Black Dragon + Quem + Cartesia + Ecclesia + Grang + Opening in gy. (5+ Disruptions)
notes: Super high ceiling endboard.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Black Dragon + Ecclesia + Quem + Opening in gy + Grang + Cartesia in gy
notes: This is the best 1cc when you dont play TSK.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Black Dragon + Quem + Ecclesia + Cartesia in gy + Opening in gy (4+ Disruptions)
notes: Best line you can do with 1 card albaz combo if you arent playing the golden swordsoul. The endboard is worse than the endboard you can make if you play swordsoul, as you can add grang to your endboard, which can be an extra disruption with luluwalillith.

endboard: Sanctifire + Black Dragon + Requiem Lock + Ecclesia + Granguinol + Opening in gy. (2+ Disruptions + Floodgate)
notes: Higher ceiling version than the standard White Albaz 1cc since it floodgates the opponent.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Black Dragon + Quem + Merc + Grang + Ecclesia + Opening in gy + Cartesia in gy (5+ Disruptions)
notes: Slightly superior to the White Albaz 1cc, since it gets merc to hand in the endphase, instead of needing to resolve Black Dragon on the opponents turn for it. Black Dragon can instead be used to revive Cartesia.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Merc (2 disruptions)
notes: Weak endboard, but playable. can be worth it to cut tragedy. Can also go for etude floodgate instead of merc.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Grang
notes: Can go for this instead of the above combo, even if you play lubellion.

endboard: Ecclesia + Retribution + Grang + White Albaz
notes: Stronger combo than the above options.

endboard: Quem + White Albaz + Ecclesia + Banishment + Mirrorjade in gy + Cart in hand + Opening in gy + Grang in gy (4+ Disruptions)
notes: Counter Intuitive endboard since it doesnt end on mirrorjade, but still very strong.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Red + White Albaz in gy + Opening in gy (3+ Disruptions)
notes: Slightly weaker endboard than version 1, but plays under nibiru.

endboard: Quem + Granguinol + Branded in red + Mirrorjade in gy + Albaz in hand + Cart in gy + Requiem Lock (1 Disruption + Floodgate)
notes: Alternative combo with Branded Fusion for if you really want to floodgate your opponent.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Red (2 Disruptions)
notes: The original combo. Doesnt work well unless you play Guardian Chimera, or Dragostapelia, or have additional monsters in hand to fuse with tragedy.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Lost + Merc + Cart in gy + Opening in gy (2+ Disruptions)
notes: Works best with 2 Quem!!!! Much worse without 2 Quem.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Banishment + Lost + Opening in gy (3 Disruptions)
notes: Up to 5 disruptions if you play LADD

endboard: Mirrorjade + Lost + Quem + Ecclesia + White Albaz + Cartesia in hand + Opening in gy (4+ Disruptions)
notes: Same as Aluber + 2 Discards combos, except requires 1 extra discard.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Lost + Banishment (3 Disruptions)
notes: The classic line before new support cards. Can still go for this combo if you dont want to spare 3 discards.

endboard: Quem + Cart + Retribution + Draw 1 (2+ Disruptions)
notes: Works best with a 2nd Quem. Cartesia fuses into Grang on the opponents turn, which then summons the 2nd Quem, sends Albaz or White Albaz to the graveyard.

endboard: Quem + Cart + White Albaz in hand + Grang in gy + Draw 1 (2+ Disruptions)
notes: Relies on Cartesia resolving to get all your disruptions moving. Works best with 2 Quem, and 2 Grang.

endboard: Quem + White Albaz + Ecclesia + Retribution + Cart in hand (3+ Disruptions)
notes: This is the ideal Nadir Servant combo post-support. There is more potential disruptions than any other Nadir combo.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Banishment + Lost (3 Disruptions)
notes: Same line as the basic Aluber combo, but requires 1 additional discard.

endboard: White Albaz + Ecclesia
notes: Can summon Albaz on the opponents turn to fuse. This is around 2 disruptions typically.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Black Dragon + Quem + Cartesia + Ecclesia + Grang + Opening in gy. (5+ Disruptions)
notes: Exactly the same as the 1 card White Albaz combo with 1 extra step.

endboard: Black Dragon + Quem + Cartesia + White Albaz in hand + Lost + Grang + Golden in hand + Ecclesia in hand (3+ Disruptions)
notes: Makes use of Seyfert second effect.

endboard: Ecclesia + Quem + Black Dragon + White Albaz in gy (3+ Disruptions)
notes: Not a great endboard but still has the potential to access all the key disruptions for Branded.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Rindbrum + Black Dragon + Ecclesia + Opening in gy + Cartesia in gy + Brafu in hand
notes: Stronger than the standard white albaz 1cc.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Rindbrum + Black Dragon + Ecclesia + Cartesia + Opening in gy
notes: REPLAY MISSES THE SYNCHRO RETURNING IN ENDPHASE.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Retribution + Quem + White Albaz + Ecclesia + Cartesia + Grang + Merc in hand (6+ disruptions)
notes: Extremely strong endboard. Should easily win most games if you make this board. Can even win games realistically if you make this board under maxx c / fuwa against most matchups.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Merc + Grang + Golden Swordsoul in hand + Cartesia + Requiem Lock (3+ Disruptions + Floodgate)
notes: Requiem Lock, so should auto-win the game in most matchups. Can also work with Kristya (or Ido in the TCG).

endboard: Mirrorjade + Branded in Red + Lost + Quem + Albaz in GY + Draw 1 (4+ Disruptions)
notes: Pre-Support combo is still very strong.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Ecclesia + Black Dragon + Cartesia + Retribution + Grang (6+ Disruptions)
notes: Very strong endboard.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Ecclesia + Black Dragon + Cartesia + Retribution + Merc + White Albaz in gy (6+ disruptions)
notes: Even stronger endboard than the above line. Also plays better into imperm and veiler, starting with Aluber baits those handtraps very well.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Ecclesia + Lost + Banishment + White Albaz + Cartesia in hand. (5+ Disruptions)
notes: Strong endboard. Sets banishment instead of Retribution or The Fallen & the Virtuous, because setting up a fusion summon is important for triggering Branded Lost to search for Merc.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Ecclesia + Etude Floodgate + White Albaz + Golden Swordsoul + Retribution (5+ Disruptions + floodgate)
notes: Greedy endboard that goes for huge amount of disruption and also Etude floodgate. Plays harder into nibiru than the other version.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Rindbrum + Retribution + Lost + Quem + Cartesia + Ecclesia + White Albaz + Magna + Opening in gy + Grang in gy (8+ Disruptions)
notes: Has 7 reliable disruptions, with the potential for even more if you play a 2nd merc.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Lost + Merc + Cartesia + Ecclesia + Black Dragon + Retribution (6+ Disruptions)
notes: Can also place Etude instead of Lost. Lost can search for Albaz to use as material for Cartesia effect, compared to Etude which floodgates the opponent.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Rindbrum + Branded in Red + Lost + Quem + Cartesia + Albaz in GY + Draw 1 (7+ Disruptions)
notes: Technically a stronger endboard than the post-support combo, but plays worse into every single handtrap.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Lost + Cartesia + Ecclesia + Black Dragon + Retribution + Merc (6+ Disruptions)
notes: Endboard also has Black Dragon returning!! Don't forget this!

endboard: Mirrorjade + Banishment + Lost + Quem + Albaz in GY (4+ Disruptions)
notes: Pre-support version. Can also do this same line post-support, but it is worse than the Synchro combo.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Ecclesia + Cart + Lost + White Albaz in hand + Black Dragon + A Bystial + Grang + Opening in gy + Draw 1 (6+ Disruptions)
notes: Very strong endboard with lots of options, disruptions, followup, and survivability.

combo: White Albaz + Branded Fusion + 1 discard (Branded Fusion version)
endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Ecclesia + Merc + Retribution + Grang + Cartesia in hand (5+ Disruptions)
notes: An alternative version to doing the regular 1 card White Albaz combo and ignoring the Branded Fusion in hand.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Black Dragon + Quem + Cartesia + Ecclesia + Grang + Opening in gy. (5+ Disruptions)
notes: Exactly the same as regular White Albaz 1cc. Just ignores the Branded Fusion in hand completely.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Ecclesia + Cartesia + Lost + Etude + The Bystial Lubellion + Black Dragon + White Albaz in hand + Saronir in hand + Merc in banishment (7+ Disruptions + Floodgate)
notes: Endboard also has Black Dragon returning in the endphase. Replay misses this.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Ecclesia + Cartesia + Lost + Dogma + Retribution + White Albaz (6+ Disruptions)
notes: Also a strong combo.

endboard: Hand loop 4 + Etude floodgate + Ecclesia
notes: Requires regained and Etude in the main deck.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Ecclsia + Cartesia + Quem + Merc + The Fallen and the Virtuous + Black Dragon (5+ Disruptions)
notes: Endboard has 5+ disruptions, but these disruptions are very very reliable, so this endboard is tricky to break.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Banishment + Albaz in gy + Draw 1 (3+ Disruptions)
notes: Best combo pre-support.

endboard: Mirrorjade + White Albaz in gy (2+ Disruptions)
notes: 2 Solid disruptions. Could potentially be more depending on what you are able to fuse into with Albaz.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Cartesia + Lost + Merc + Black Dragon + Ecclesia + Grang in gy + Opening in gy (5+ Disruptions)
notes: Does not activate Albion effect for two reasons. Firstly, there is no good monster to summon with it. Secondly, you will run out of space if you summon another monster.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Banishment + Lost + Quem + Albaz in gy + Cart in hand
notes: Doesnt use the normal summon.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Ecclesia + Cartesia + Merc + Grang + Black Dragon + Opening in gy (5+ Disruptions)
notes: Doesnt use the normal summon.

endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Banishment + Lost
notes: Decent endboard. Typical endboard for pre-support branded.

starter: High Spirits + Any Dragon / Winged Beast / Beast / Spellcaster
endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Ecclesia + Cartesia + Black Dragon + Merc + Grang + Opening in gy (5+ Disruptions)
notes: This is pretty much exactly the same combo as High Spirits + Saronir, but missing the saronir send.

starter: High Spirits + Any Dragon / Winged Beast / Beast / Spellcaster
combo: High Spirits + Any winged beast target
endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Ecclesia + Cartesia + Black Dragon + Grang + Opening in gy (5+ Disruptions)

starter: High Spirits + Any Dragon / Winged Beast / Beast / Spellcaster
endboard: Mirrorjade + Quem + Ecclesia + Cartesia + Black Dragon + Grang + Opening in gy (5+ Disruptions)

endboard: Mirrorjade + Banishment + Lost + Quem + Albaz in GY

endboard: Mirrorjade + Branded in Red + Lost + Quem + Albaz in GY + Draw 1
Mirrorjade + Rindbrum + Quem + Cart + Banishment + Lost + Albaz in hand + Granguinol in gy + Magna

Mirrorjade + Lost + Branded in Red + Albaz in gy + Granguinol in gy + Cart in hand + Draw 1

endboard: Quem + Cart + Albaz in hand + Albaz in gy + Granguinol in gy + Draw 2

endboard: Mirrorjade + Banishment + Lost + Quem + Cart + Albaz in gy + Draw 1
